mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easier discount-rate regime to equities; when the policy rate is not rising, lower short rates support higher S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend signals inflation pressure that raises expected future policy restraint and equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL inflation spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if the forward-return difference versus non-trigger periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE accompanied by slowing PAYEMS growth marks labor-market deterioration and lower earnings expectations; this combination predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS deterioration does not outperform either series alone and the triggered sample does not show significantly negative 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A broad sales slowdown in RSAFS indicates weaker consumer demand and lower near-term revenue growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the sales series turns down sharply.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal is indistinguishable from random over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects weaker industrial output and softer cyclically sensitive earnings; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if downward moves in INDPRO do not precede weaker 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling correlation between INDPRO changes and forward SPX returns turns persistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST following prior strength signals tightening housing demand and a future slowdown in spending and financial conditions; that should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 8% from its 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the drawdown signal loses significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income and consumption expectations deteriorate together, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE pattern does not generate worse 60-day SPX returns than either proxy separately, or if sign accuracy falls below 50% in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with elevated CPIAUCSL should signal policy pressure and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if, during periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, elevated CPIAUCSL does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is not stronger than either series alone.
